questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: In the context of regional cooperation, how does the 'SAFE Agromet Project' differ from the 'ASEAN Food Security Information System (AFSIS)'?

options.text: AFSIS provides reliable information on food security, while SAFE Agromet uses space technology to understand environmental and climate changes.
AFSIS is a maritime security initiative, while SAFE Agromet is focused on agricultural trade tariffs.
AFSIS is led by India, whereas SAFE Agromet is led exclusively by Thailand.
AFSIS focuses on digital connectivity in the North East, while SAFE Agromet focuses on deep-sea mining.

questions.explanation: AFSIS is a project led by Thailand to provide reliable food security information. The SAFE Agromet Project (implemented by ISRO and others) uses space technology to monitor environmental and climate change issues related to agriculture.
questions.takeaway: ASEAN-India cooperation in food security leverages both information systems (AFSIS) and space-based technological monitoring (SAFE Agromet).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: AFSIS is a project led by Thailand to provide the reliable information on food security. SAFE Project was established by Asia-Pacific Regional Space Agency in 2008 which uses space technology in understanding environmental changes and climate change related issues.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.prompt: According to the ASEAN Charter, which of the following best describes the principle of 'ASEAN Centrality'?

options.text: The requirement that all regional security dialogues must be chaired by a founding member of ASEAN.
The mandate that ASEAN must be the primary driving force in its relations and cooperation with external partners.
The geographical focus of the Indo-Pacific strategy on the South China Sea.
The economic dominance of the five founding members over the newer members of the bloc.

questions.explanation: Article 1.15 of the ASEAN Charter emphasizes upholding the centrality and active role of ASEAN as the main driving force behind its relations and cooperation with external partners.
questions.takeaway: ASEAN Centrality is a formal Charter-based principle ensuring the bloc remains the leader in regional architecture and external engagements.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Article 1.15 of the ASEAN Charter emphasises that the grouping’s primary goal is to uphold the centrality and active role of ASEAN as the main driving force behind its relations and cooperation with external partners.
